The TASKI GO is the entry point to professional TASKI floorcare - a simple, robust tub vacuum built for operations that need dependable daily cleaning without a premium price tag.

Its 900 W motor and smart motor positioning deliver strong 22 kPa suction and 44 litres per second of airflow, comfortably handling high-frequency commercial work.

TASKI deliberately rationalised the part count, which keeps both reliability high and servicing cheap - a sensible choice for short contracts, remote sites or as a fleet machine where replacement cost matters more than features. It is also built from 75 percent recycled plastic and is RoHS and REACH compliant.

Key Features

  • 16 litre canister taking an 8 litre paper dust bag
  • 900 W motor with smart motor positioning
  • 22 kPa vacuum and 44 l/s maximum airflow
  • Made from 75 percent recycled plastic; RoHS and REACH compliant
  • Rationalised part count for reliability and low servicing cost
  • Three-stage filtration
  • 8.5 m cable and 2 m hose; metal suction tubes
  • 5.7 kg with cable; protection class II

name: Taski Aero BP E UK
short_description: Corded backpack vacuum weighing just 4.7 kg, converting to a compact tub vacuum or a blower, for stairs, lifts and seated areas where wheeled machines struggle.
description:
The TASKI AERO BP E is a corded professional backpack vacuum built for the places wheeled machines struggle - stairwells, lifts, entrance lobbies, auditoriums and rows of fixed seating.

At 4.7 kg it is among the lightest in its class, and the slim profile with rucksack webbing distributes that load across the operator's back rather than hanging off the shoulders, which matters over a full shift.

Its real flexibility is the three-in-one design. Remove four bolts and the harness comes off, four caster wheels screw in, and you have an ultra-compact tub vacuum. Fit the blower plate instead and it clears leaves, sand and debris from outdoor areas. With 700 W driving 30 litres per second of airflow and a 12.5 m cable, one machine covers indoor detail work, open-floor vacuuming and outdoor clearing.

Key Features

  • 700 W motor with 30 l/s airflow - the strongest in the BP family
  • Just 4.7 kg ready to use
  • Three-in-one: backpack vacuum, tub vacuum and blower
  • Slim profile with rucksack webbing for even weight distribution
  • 4.5 litre three-layer filter bag; three-stage filtration
  • HEPA available as an option
  • 12.5 m cable; protection class II
  • Ideal for stairs, lifts, entrances and tiered seating

Conversion parts are separate: wheel conversion set 7524499, blower plate 7524574.

name: Taski Aero BP B Li-Ion
short_description: Cordless Li-Ion backpack vacuum with 30 minutes runtime and five-stage filtration, sharing its 36 V battery and charger with the TASKI swingo 150 B scrubber drier.
description:
The TASKI AERO BP B Li-Ion is the cordless member of the AERO backpack family, built for environments where a trailing cable is simply not acceptable - transport hubs, building circulation areas, leisure venues and anywhere the public is moving around the operator.

With no cable to manage, cleaners can spot-clean or move between zones freely, and daytime cleaning becomes practical without cordoning off areas.

A 36 V 6 Ah lithium battery gives 30 minutes of continuous work and recharges in 90 minutes, with spare batteries available for back-to-back shifts. Crucially it shares its battery and charger with the TASKI swingo 150 B scrubber drier, so operations running both machines standardise on one battery pool. Five-stage filtration and an optional HEPA element make it suitable for air-quality-sensitive sites, and like its corded sibling it converts to a compact tub vacuum or a debris blower.

  • Quietest of the BP range at 61 dB
  • 4.5 litre three-layer filter bag
  • Converts to a compact tub vacuum or a blower
  • 5.3 kg ready to use with battery; insulation class III

name: Taski Vacumat 22 UK
short_description: Professional wet and dry tub vacuum with a 1,000 W motor and 22 litre wet capacity, running at 64 dB(A) for cleaning in occupied areas.
description:
The TASKI vacumat 22 is a professional tub vacuum built for operators who need one machine to handle both spillages and routine dry vacuuming.

Its 1,000 W motor produces 60 litres per second of airflow at 22 kPa, giving fast pick-up of standing water, while a 22-litre wet (28-litre dry) container means fewer trips to empty. A mechanical float system paired with a dual filter shuts off suction before liquid can reach the motor, protecting the unit and extending service life, and the canister resists both impact and cleaning chemicals.

Flexibility is the machine's core strength. Fitting the optional dry vacuuming kit turns it into a full dry vacuum for hard and soft floors, and the accessory range extends it to stair cleaning and vertical surfaces. It also pairs with TASKI single-disc machines for water recovery after scrubbing. At 64 dB(A) and 10.5 kg it stays quiet and manoeuvrable enough for occupied premises.

Key Features

  • Wet and dry capability: 22 litres wet, 28 litres dry (45 litre total container volume)
  • 1,000 W motor delivering 60 l/s airflow and 22 kPa vacuum
  • Quiet 64 dB(A) operation, suitable for daytime cleaning in occupied areas
  • Mechanical float shut-off with dual filter for double motor protection
  • Shock- and chemical-resistant canister
  • 10 m cable and 2.2 m suction hose; five 50 mm castors
  • Converts to dry vacuuming with the optional dry vacuum kit
  • Protection class II

Supplied without accessories - nozzle and hose kits are ordered separately.

name: Taski Vacuum Cleaner Vacumat 44T UK
short_description: Large twin-motor wet vacuum on a fixed trolley with a 44 litre container, designed to pair with a 64 cm fixomat squeegee for fast water recovery over large areas.
description:
The TASKI vacumat 44T is the large-capacity workhorse of the vacumat range, built around a fixed trolley (the “T”) and designed to run a 64 cm fixomat front-mounted squeegee.

Twin 950 W motors deliver 2 × 50 litres per second of airflow at 2 × 22 kPa, and the wide squeegee lets a single operator recover water from medium-to-large floor areas up to three times faster than conventional wet vacuuming. It is the natural partner to a TASKI ergodisc single-disc machine for stripping and wet scrubbing, where the volume of solution to recover is high.

A 44-litre container (70 litres total volume) keeps the machine working for long periods between emptying, and a tilt system with a moulded handle grip makes that emptying manageable. The adjustable push handle, 200 mm wheels and 15 m cable suit long runs across large sites. A mechanical float system backed by a dual filter gives the motors double protection.

Key Features

  • Twin motors: 2 × 950 W, 2 × 50 l/s airflow, 2 × 22 kPa vacuum
  • 44 litre container capacity (70 litre total volume)
  • Fixed trolley chassis with adjustable push handle
  • Takes the 64 cm fixomat squeegee for up to 3× faster water recovery
  • Tilt system and moulded handle grip for easier emptying
  • Mechanical float with dual filter for double motor protection
  • Shock- and chemical-resistant canister
  • 15 m cable; 2 × 200 mm wheels plus 2 × 75 mm castors
  • 67 dB(A); protection class II

Supplied with the trolley but without the fixomat squeegee, which is ordered separately as part 8505420.

short_description: 43 cm disc drive for TASKI ergodisc single-disc machines, holding a floor pad in place of a brush.
description:
The disc drive is the pad-holder plate that mounts under an ergodisc single-disc machine so it can run floor pads instead of a brush.

Brushes screw straight onto the machine's coupling; pads cannot, so the disc drive is what makes pad work possible. It is the standard-speed version - for high-speed polishing on the ergodisc duo or 400, the high-speed disc drive (part 7510030) is the correct part.

Important: this is an ergodisc part. The 43 cm pad driver for swingo scrubber driers (part 7510829) is a different, non-interchangeable component despite sharing the same diameter.

name: Taski Water Tank for Ergodisc Single Disc Machines
short_description: Attachable solution tank for TASKI ergodisc single-disc machines, feeding cleaning solution through the centre of the brush. Fits ergodisc 165, HD and duo.
description:
The water tank is the accessory that turns an ergodisc from a buffing and polishing machine into a scrubbing machine.

Without it, solution has to be spread on the floor manually before the disc passes over it - wasteful, uneven and slow. With it, solution is metered through the centre of the brush directly into the working zone, so chemical consumption drops and cleaning is consistent across the pass.

The tank clips onto the machine body without tools, in keeping with the rest of the ergodisc accessory system, and has a large fill opening for fast refills from a bucket or hose. TASKI's own literature calls it the best accessory for wet scrubbing and deep cleaning, so it is best treated as a companion purchase alongside the machine rather than an optional extra.

Key Features

  • Attaches to the machine without tools
  • Feeds solution through the centre of the brush, where the disc is working
  • Large fill opening for fast refills without a funnel
  • Converts a dry single-disc machine into a wet-scrubbing machine
  • Fits across the ergodisc range, so tanks can be shared between machines in a fleet

Compatibility

TASKI ergodisc 165, ergodisc HD and ergodisc duo per the official accessories chart; distributor sources additionally list ergodisc 175, 200 and 400.

name: Taski Floor Scrubber Ergodisc 165 UK
short_description: Low-speed 43 cm single-disc floor machine running at 165 rpm, covering wet scrubbing, stripping, buffing, spray cleaning, shampooing and crystallisation.
description:
The TASKI ergodisc 165 is the workhorse of TASKI's single-disc line: one machine, one 43 cm disc, and a full accessory ecosystem that lets it switch between scrubbing, stripping, polishing and carpet shampooing without tools.

At 165 rpm it sits at the low-speed end of the range, which is exactly what heavy wet work needs - stripping old polish, deep-scrubbing textured floors, and driving abrasive pads without burning the surface. Shock-absorbing housings give it an extended service life and low service cost, which is why the ergodisc name has held its position in contract cleaning for decades.

Paired with the water tank accessory it becomes a wet-scrubbing machine; with the suction unit and skirt it controls dust during spray cleaning; with the foam generator it does dry-foam carpet care. Being mains-powered means no battery replacement cycle and no charging downtime.

Key Features

  • 43 cm working width at 165 rpm, driven by an 1,100 W motor
  • Seven task modes: wet scrubbing, stripping, buffing, spray cleaning, shampooing, wood treatment and crystallisation
  • Tool-free accessory attachment
  • Foldable handle and removable 5 kg weight for storage and transport
  • Soft-start motor with low vibration (under 0.5 m/s²)
  • Quiet 57 dB(A) operation, suitable for occupied-hours work
  • Double insulation (protection class II) with closed handle and double safety lock
  • 15 m cable for a large uninterrupted working radius
  • Two-year warranty

This is a single-disc rotary machine, not a scrubber-dryer - it has no recovery tank or squeegee. The water tank (part 8504390) is an optional accessory required for wet scrubbing.

name: Taski Foam Generator FG2
short_description: Mobile foam generator with an onboard compressor and 5.5 litre tank, turning liquid chemical into clinging foam with adjustable consistency.
description:
The TASKI FG2 is a portable foam generator that turns liquid cleaning or disinfection chemical into a clinging foam, so the product stays on vertical and overhead surfaces long enough to work rather than running straight off.

It carries its own electric compressor and a 5.5-litre solution tank, so it needs only a mains socket - no shop air line. Foam consistency, from wet through to dry, is set on the machine using the foam regulator and dosing valve, letting the same unit serve carpet dry-foam work, washroom and kitchen surface cleaning, and equipment disinfection.

It is built as a service machine: the internal pressure tank, compressor, filter, dosing valve, magnetic valve and check valves are all individually replaceable, and a full spare-parts list is still published and maintained. At 120 W and 62 dB(A) it is quiet and cheap to run.

Key Features

  • Onboard electric compressor - no external air supply needed
  • 5.5 litre solution tank
  • Adjustable foam consistency via foam regulator and dosing valve
  • Hose, wand and nozzle set for walls, ceilings and equipment
  • Quiet at 62 dB(A), low draw at 120 W
  • IPX4 splash protection, class II double insulated
  • Individually replaceable service components

name: Taski Ergodisc Duo
short_description: Dual-speed 43 cm single-disc floor machine switching between 165 rpm for scrubbing and stripping and 330 rpm for buffing and polishing - two machines in one chassis.
description:
The TASKI ergodisc duo exists to solve a specific procurement problem: most sites need both a low-speed machine for stripping and scrubbing and a high-speed machine for buffing and polishing, and buying two machines doubles the capital cost, the storage footprint and the training burden.

The duo collapses that into one chassis with a two-speed gearbox - TASKI markets it plainly as two powerful machines in one. At 165 rpm it handles heavy wet scrubbing, stripping, deep cleaning and grinding; at 330 rpm it buffs, spray cleans, polishes and crystallises.

For a contract cleaner or hotel running mixed floor types - terrazzo and marble that need crystallisation and polishing, plus vinyl and tile that need periodic stripping and re-sealing - the duo is the most flexible machine in the ergodisc range. Note the trade-off: it is meaningfully heavier than the ergodisc 165 because of the gearbox, which is felt when carrying it between floors.

Key Features

  • Two switchable speeds from one 43 cm disc: 165 rpm and 330 rpm
  • Eight task modes - the widest of any ergodisc, adding grinding
  • Higher brush pressure than the ergodisc 165 (0.4 vs 0.3 N/cm²) for more aggressive stripping
  • 1,100 W motor on 230 V single phase
  • Anatomically formed grip with adjustable handlebar height
  • Soft start, low vibration, 57 dB(A)
  • Double insulation (protection class II), closed handle, double safety lock
  • 15 m cable, foldable handle, removable 5 kg weight
  • Two-year warranty

This is a single-disc machine with a two-speed gearbox - not a twin-disc machine. It has no recovery tank or squeegee; the water tank (part 8504390) is an optional accessory for wet scrubbing.

name: Taski Sprayer BP 15 Li-Ion
short_description: Battery backpack sprayer with a 15 litre tank and four hours of runtime, adjustable to 3 bar with two nozzles for fine misting or heavier wet application.
description:
The TASKI Sprayer BP15 Li-Ion pairs a conventional, well-proven backpack sprayer with an 18 V lithium-ion battery, removing the constant hand-pumping that limits how much ground one operator can cover.

You switch it on, put it on your back, and spray continuously for up to four hours on one charge, then recharge in 80 minutes. Pressure is adjustable up to 3 bar, and two supplied nozzles give 60-micron and 200-micron droplet sizes, so the same machine handles fine disinfection misting and heavier wet application of cleaning chemicals.

It is built in Switzerland around a stainless steel lance and a diaphragm pump, which is what allows it to be run with a broad range of aggressive cleaning and disinfection products rather than water alone. At 4.5 kg loaded weight excluding solution and under 70 dB(A), it is comfortable for extended wear and quiet enough for occupied premises.

Key Features

  • 15 litre tank with adjustable pressure up to 3 bar
  • 18 V 2.0 Ah lithium-ion battery giving 4 hours runtime, 80 minute recharge
  • Two nozzles supplied: 60 micron and 200 micron droplet sizes
  • Stainless steel spray lance, chemical resistant
  • Diaphragm pump handles a wide range of cleaning and disinfection chemicals
  • Only 4.5 kg with battery; under 70 dB(A)
  • Made in Switzerland; CE approved
  • Ships ready to use with battery and charger included

name: Taski Aquamat 20.01 UK
short_description: Spray-extraction machine for carpets, upholstery and hard floors, with separate 22 litre solution and 18 litre recovery tanks and variable product dosing.
description:
Deep cleaning by spray extraction is the most thorough way to clean carpet - it lifts ingrained dirt that vacuuming alone leaves behind, extending carpet life while restoring appearance.

The TASKI aquamat 20 is built for exactly this: a 22-litre solution tank and separate 18-litre recovery tank give it the endurance for small-to-mid-sized congested areas such as hotel corridors, offices, schools and shopping centres, while variable product dosing lets the operator increase chemical concentration on heavily soiled sections rather than treating the whole floor the same way.

A 1,050 W vacuum motor pulling 47 litres per second at 22 kPa recovers solution quickly for shorter drying times, and it is strong enough to run an optional 6-metre hose - so the machine can sit at the bottom of a stairwell or outside the room while the operator works. Fitted with the hard floor tool set instead, the same machine cleans ceramic tile and anti-slip flooring, making it genuinely multi-functional.

Key Features

  • Separate tanks: 22 litres solution, 18 litres recovery
  • 1,050 W motor, 47 l/s airflow, 22 kPa vacuum
  • 25 cm working width

  • Optional 12 cm hand nozzle for upholstery and stairs
  • Optional carpet pile brush lifts fibres and reduces drying time
  • 15 m cable; 66 dB(A); protection class II

Supplied without accessories. The spray extraction tool set (part 8505160) is required for operation and is ordered separately.

name: Taski Swingo 250 Micro
short_description: Ultra-compact 44 cm battery scrubber dryer built to replace the mop and bucket, covering 1,320 square metres an hour with hot-swappable lithium batteries.
description:
The TASKI swingo 250µicro attacks the single largest inefficiency in commercial cleaning - the mop. A mop redeposits dirty water, leaves floors wet and hazardous for minutes, and delivers inconsistent results.

The 250µicro fits the same operational niche - grab it, clean a small area, put it away - while scrubbing with twin counter-rotating brushes and vacuuming the soil straight back off the floor. At 44 cm wide and 25 kg it goes where a conventional scrubber dryer cannot: behind retail counters, into washrooms, through office aisles, up stairs to a mezzanine.

The economics are about labour and water. One operator covers 1,320 m² an hour instead of the few hundred a mop manages, and each 2.2 litre tank fill treats over 600 m² - a fraction of the water a mop bucket consumes, with the dirty water captured in a sealed recovery tank rather than poured back onto the floor. Two hot-swappable batteries mean a full shift with no downtime. The honest trade-off: the tanks are genuinely small, so this is a machine for frequent small-area cleaning, not for scrubbing a warehouse.

Key Features

  • 44 cm working width delivering 1,320 m²/h
  • Twin contra-rotating brushes at 140 rpm - counter-rotation cancels side-pull so the machine tracks straight
  • 52 cm squeegee leaves floors walkable immediately
  • Hot-swappable 37 V lithium batteries: 60-80 minutes each, 160 minutes with both supplied
  • Charges to 80 percent in 1 hour, full in 2 hours; 2,500 cycle battery life
  • Adjustable water flow 70-330 ml/min, over 600 m² per tank fill
  • Only 25 kg ready to use - one person can carry it upstairs
  • 68.5 dB(A) or less in Eco mode for daytime cleaning
  • Starter Kit ships complete with machine, two batteries, charger, two brushes and squeegee

name: Taski Balimat 45 Sweeper
short_description: Manual push sweeper with twin counter-rotating brushes and a 40 litre hopper, sweeping up to five times faster than a broom with no power, batteries or fuel.
description:
The TASKI balimat 45 is a purely mechanical push sweeper built for operators who need broom-replacement productivity without any running costs.

Pushing the machine drives twin counter-rotating cylindrical brushes, while the side brushes sweep debris in from kerbs, corners and edges into the sweeping path. Everything is thrown into a 40-litre detachable hopper that lifts out for emptying. Because there is no motor, battery or cable, it starts working the moment it is picked up and can be used in wet conditions where a vacuum sweeper could not.

At 13 kg it is light enough for one person to carry up steps or lift into a vehicle, and the handle folds down so it stores in a cupboard. It suits shop entrances, hotel forecourts, school corridors, warehouse aprons and building perimeters - anywhere a broom is currently used over a moderate area.

Key Features

  • Fully manual - no power, batteries or fuel
  • Up to five times faster than a conventional broom
  • Twin counter-rotating cylindrical main brushes with side brushes funnelling debris inward
  • 470 mm main brush path, up to 800 mm with both side brushes fitted
  • 40 litre detachable waste hopper
  • Works indoors and outdoors, including in wet conditions
  • Just 13 kg, with a folding handle for compact storage
  • Quick-connect side brushes for tool-free replacement

name: Taski Balimat 3300 RTU Sweeper
short_description: Battery walk-behind sweeper with a 900 mm sweeping path and 60 litre hopper, covering 3,300 square metres an hour with up to 3.5 hours of runtime.
description:
The TASKI balimat 3300 is the largest walk-behind machine in the balimat range, built for car parks, warehouses, loading bays, forecourts and large public areas where a push sweeper would be too slow.

A 900 mm sweeping path combined with a 60-litre hopper gives a theoretical performance of 3,300 m²/h, and battery operation with brush assist means the operator guides the machine rather than pushing it.

Sweeping quality comes from TASKI's patented Tandem Roller System: two counter-rotating roller brushes hand debris from one roller to the other and throw it upward, while a shaped guide plate above the roller intersection redirects the dirt backwards into the container. The offset side broom clears edges and corners into the roller path, so a single pass is enough. At 62 dB(A) it is quiet enough for daytime use in occupied buildings, and it arrives ready to use with no operator training required.

Key Features

  • 900 mm sweeping path with a 60 litre hopper
  • 3,300 m²/h theoretical area performance
  • Patented Tandem Roller System for a single-pass result
  • Dual side brooms sweeping in from edges and corners
  • Battery operation with brush assist, up to 3.5 hours autonomy
  • Works indoors and outdoors, on hard floors and carpet
  • Side broom and roller brushes adjust without tools

short_description: Genuine 12 V sealed gel traction battery block, fitted to the TASKI balimat 3300 sweeper and to swingo 755B, 855B, 955 and 1255 scrubber driers.
description:
A sealed gel traction battery block used across several TASKI machines, supplying drive and brush motors.

Gel construction is sealed and maintenance-free, so there is no topping up with distilled water and no acid to spill - which matters on machines that get tipped and manoeuvred in occupied buildings.

Compatibility

TASKI balimat 3300 walk-behind sweeper (1 block). TASKI swingo 755B Eco, swingo 755B Power, swingo 855B and swingo 955 (2 blocks each); swingo 1255 (4 blocks).

Key Details

  • 12 V sealed gel traction block
  • Maintenance-free - no watering, no spillable acid
  • Supports up to 3.5 hours of sweeping autonomy on the balimat 3300
  • Genuine TASKI part 7520152

Note: TASKI's sweeper accessory chart lists this block at 81 Ah while the scrubber-drier charts list it at 76 Ah. Confirm the rating with your distributor if exact capacity matters for your application.

name: Center Broom B3300
short_description: Genuine replacement centre roller broom for the TASKI balimat 3300 walk-behind sweeper.
description:
The centre broom is the main sweeping roller on the TASKI balimat 3300. It forms half of the machine's patented Tandem Roller System, where two counter-rotating rollers hand debris between them and throw it up into the hopper.

Roller condition directly determines sweeping quality - worn bristles no longer reach into floor texture or lift heavier debris, so a single pass stops being enough. Replacement is tool-free.

Compatibility

TASKI balimat 3300 walk-behind sweeper only. The balimat 2300 and 6500 take different brooms.

Key Details

  • 500 mm main brush width
  • Adjusts and replaces without tools
  • Genuine TASKI part 7524909

short_description: Genuine replacement side broom for the TASKI balimat 3300 walk-behind sweeper, sweeping debris in from edges and corners.
description:
The side broom sweeps debris in from kerbs, edges and corners into the path of the main roller, which is what lets the balimat 3300 clean right up to a wall rather than leaving a strip behind.

It is a wear item - bristles shorten and splay with use, and once they no longer reach past the machine body the edge-cleaning benefit is lost. Replacement is tool-free.

name: Taski Swingo 2100 Micro BMS UK
short_description: Compact micro ride-on scrubber dryer with a 55 cm deck that extends 14 cm sideways to clean hard against walls, covering 3,025 square metres an hour.
description:
The TASKI swingo 2100µicro exists because of a gap in most cleaning fleets: walk-behind scrubbers are too slow for large floors, but conventional ride-ons are too big to get into the spaces those floors actually contain - lifts, aisles, checkouts, doorways.

TASKI's answer was to shrink the ride-on rather than speed up the walk-behind. The result covers 3,025 m² an hour with a 75-litre tank, yet fits in a standard passenger lift and can be moved between floors of a shopping centre or hospital without a goods lift.

The retractable offset deck is the feature that changes the operational maths. On most ride-ons, an operator finishes the open floor and then goes back with a mop to do the edges - often a fifth to a third of the total time. The 2100µicro extends its cleaning head 14 cm sideways to reach right up to walls and shelf lines, so edge work happens in the same pass. Combined with around four hours of battery runtime and IntelliDose automatic chemical dosing, this is a machine aimed squarely at lowering total cost of ownership on large retail, healthcare and logistics floors.

Key Features

  • 55 cm working width from twin 28 cm discs at 190 rpm
  • Retractable offset deck extends 14 cm sideways to clean against walls and under shelving
  • 3,025 m²/h at up to 5.5 km/h; around 5,000 m² per tank fill
  • 75 litre solution and 75 litre recovery tanks
  • 24 V battery system with up to four hours runtime; gel or lithium options
  • 63 cm squeegee
  • Under 70 dB(A) in Eco mode for daytime retail and hospital use
  • Fits standard lifts and doorways at 138 × 58 × 128 cm
  • Supports IntelliDose, IntelliFlow, IntelliTrail and IntelliSpray

description:
OEM Sheffield Chrome Wire Shelving – 1220mm

The OEM Sheffield Chrome Wire Shelving at 1220mm is a professional-grade heavy-duty wire shelving unit that combines strength, ventilation, and versatility for commercial storage environments. The chrome-plated wire construction promotes excellent airflow around stored goods - reducing moisture build-up, inhibiting mould, and keeping stored items fresher for longer. At 1220mm wide, this unit offers maximum storage capacity for busy commercial kitchens, storerooms, and food preparation areas.

Key Features:

  • Chrome-Plated Wire Construction: Durable chrome finish provides corrosion resistance and a clean, professional appearance
  • 1220mm Width: Full-width shelving for maximum commercial storage capacity
  • Open Wire Design: Promotes excellent air circulation around stored goods for freshness and hygiene
  • Heavy-Duty Load Capacity: Robust wire gauge and frame construction for heavy commercial storage loads
  • Adjustable Shelf Positions: Flexible height configuration to suit different product storage requirements
  • Easy to Clean: Open wire design allows easy cleaning and inspection of shelves
  • Versatile Application: Suitable for dry storage, general storerooms, kitchen preparation areas, and back-of-house storage
  • Modular Design: Can be combined with additional units for larger storage configurations

Perfect For:

Commercial kitchens, hotel storerooms, restaurants, catering companies, supermarket back-of-house areas, and food service operations across Kenya that need high-capacity, well-ventilated, heavy-duty wire shelving for organized and hygienic storage.

name: Hood Type Dishwasher
description:
The HATTON HT-Z1 is a high-performance restaurant hood type dishwasher engineered for commercial kitchens that require fast, efficient cleaning solutions. This professional-grade unit combines powerful washing capabilities with water efficiency to meet the demands of busy food service operations.

﻿This commercial dishwasher provides exceptional cleaning performance while maintaining compact dimensions of 690mm × 780mm × 1475mm. With its 505mm entrance width and 420mm maximum washing height, the HT-Z1 accommodates a wide variety of dishware and kitchen utensils. This durable and reliable dishwasher is the perfect solution for restaurants, hotels, and catering services seeking to enhance their kitchen efficiency.

Key Features:

  • Single door disinfection cabinet with convenient front-open access
  • Multi-function jet-style cleaning system for superior results
  • Impressive 60 rack/hour washing capacity
  • Water-efficient design using only 3 liters per rack
  • Generous 21.5L water tank capacity
  • CE and ISO9001 certified for quality assurance
